roleArchetype.description: Short phrase classifying what kind of role this actually is, judged from the whole posting (not just the title), e.g. 'production ML/platform infrastructure leadership' or 'GPU/SoC pre-silicon validation methodology'.

coverageMatrix.description: The role's core hard requirements (the must-haves the posting treats as required), each rated against the candidate's evidence. Build this BEFORE assigning the score.

requirement.description: A core hard requirement from the posting (read from the full description even when the structured requirements list is sparse).

coverage.description: direct = specific demonstrated experience with that exact thing; adjacent = related/transferable but not the same; missing = no real evidence in the profile.

evidence.description: The concrete candidate evidence supporting this rating, or a note that it is absent.

criticalGaps.description: The missing or only-adjacent requirements that matter most for this role.

score.description: Overall fit score 0-100, consistent with the coverageMatrix. 0=clearly wrong role, 50=ambiguous, 100=ideal match.

suitability.description: Bucketed verdict consistent with score band.

confidence.description: Confidence in this score: high only if the posting is detailed and the match is clear-cut; low if the posting is vague or the evidence is mixed.

scoreRationale.description: One or two sentences tying the score to the coverage matrix (e.g. '3 of 6 core requirements are missing, so capped at Good fit').

matchedReasons.description: Concrete reasons (citing the candidate's background) why the role is a fit.

gapReasons.description: Concrete missing/weak areas relative to the role.

verdict.description: One-paragraph synthesis the candidate can read at a glance.
